groups.title: Build and Maintain a Secure Network and Systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Configuration standards are defined and implemented for all network security controls (NSCs), including firewalls and routers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Inbound traffic to the cardholder data environment (CDE) is restricted to only necessary traffic, and all other traffic is specifically denied.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Outbound traffic from the cardholder data environment (CDE) is restricted to only necessary traffic, and all other traffic is specifically denied.

controls.title: NSCs Between Trusted and Untrusted Networks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: NSCs are implemented between trusted and untrusted networks to control traffic into and out of the CDE.

groups.title: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Configuration standards are developed, implemented, and maintained to cover all system components, address all known security vulnerabilities, and be consistent with industry-accepted system hardening standards.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Vendor default accounts are managed: removed if not needed, or passwords changed if accounts are needed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All unnecessary functionality is removed or disabled, including unnecessary scripts, drivers, features, subsystems, file systems, and web servers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PAN is secured with strong cryptography wherever it is stored, using one-way hashes, truncation, index tokens, or strong cryptographic keys with associated key management.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cryptographic keys used to protect stored account data are managed securely with documented key management processes and procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A data retention and disposal policy, procedures, and processes are defined, documented, and implemented for all storage of account data.

groups.title: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission over open, public networks. Only trusted keys and certificates are accepted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: PAN is secured with strong cryptography whenever it is sent via end-user messaging technologies (email, instant messaging, SMS, chat).

groups.title: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An anti-malware solution is deployed on all system components that are commonly affected by malware, except for system components identified and evaluated that are not at risk.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The anti-malware solution is kept current via automatic updates, performs periodic and active or real-time scans, and generates audit logs.

groups.title: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Bespoke and custom software are developed securely based on industry standards and/or best practices for secure development.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security vulnerabilities are identified and managed through a defined process including vulnerability ranking and prioritization.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For public-facing web applications, new threats and vulnerabilities are addressed on an ongoing basis and applications are protected against known attacks.

groups.title: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An access control model is defined and includes granting access based on job classification and function, with default deny-all and allow-by-exception.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access is assigned to users based on job classification, function, and least privileges necessary.

groups.title: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All users are assigned a unique ID before access to system components or cardholder data is allowed.

controls.title: Strong Authentication for Users and Admins

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All user access to system components for users and administrators is authenticated via at least one of: something you know, something you have, or something you are.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented for all access into the cardholder data environment (CDE).

groups.title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Appropriate facility entry controls are in place to restrict physical access to systems in the cardholder data environment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All media with cardholder data is physically secured and access is restricted to authorized personnel.

groups.title: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Audit logs are enabled and active for all system components and cardholder data access, capturing all auditable events.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Audit log entries include: user identification, type of event, date and time, success/failure indication, origination of event, and identity/name of affected data, component, or resource.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Audit logs are reviewed at least daily to identify anomalies or suspicious activity using automated mechanisms.

groups.title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Internal vulnerability scans are performed at least quarterly and after significant changes. High-risk and critical vulnerabilities are resolved and rescans confirm resolution.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A penetration testing methodology is defined, documented, and implemented that includes industry-accepted approaches, coverage for the entire CDE perimeter and critical systems.

groups.title: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An overall information security policy is established, published, maintained, and disseminated to all relevant personnel and relevant third parties.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A formal security awareness program is implemented to make all personnel aware of the cardholder data security policy and their role in protecting cardholder data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An incident response plan is established and ready to be activated immediately in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident, including specific containment activities for different types of incidents.
